purpose: Registry of the harness control system: guides (feedforward) and sensors (feedback) across the quality axes, plus the cadence each runs at and whether it is active, partial, or planned. This file is the machine-readable companion to HARNESS.md and the single source of truth for 'what governs what' and 'where the holes are'. Keep it faithful to reality: every active/partial entry must point at a real wired_at path.

model.definition: Agent = Model + Harness. The harness is everything around the model: guides that steer it before it acts, sensors that detect after it acts, and a human steering loop that improves both over time.

axes.maintainability: Internal quality — keep the codebase easy and low-risk to change.
axes.architecture: Architecture fitness — structural constraints, layering, module boundaries, performance budgets.
axes.behaviour: Functional correctness — does it do what was asked, verified against a running system.
axes.traceability: Harness extension beyond the source articles: deterministic grounding of every artifact against its upstream (FRD -> BRD -> spec -> test). This is a strength we have that the articles' examples lack.

kinds.feedforward: A guide. Prevents or steers behaviour before the agent acts.
kinds.computational: A sensor that is deterministic, fast, CPU-based (linters, type checkers, structural rules, tests).
kinds.inferential: A sensor that uses an LLM for semantic judgement (review agents). Slower, probabilistic, semantically rich.
kinds.hybrid: A sensor that combines a deterministic runner with inferential interpretation.

cadences.planning: Runs during BRD/spec/design, before code exists.
cadences.session: Runs inside the agent's inner loop, on every write (PostToolUse / PreToolUse hooks). Fast self-correction.
cadences.commit: Runs at commit time (git pre-commit hook) or in the on-demand /gate.
cadences.integration: Runs against a running application (the evaluator / /evaluate) or in CI.
cadences.drift: Runs repeatedly on a slower cadence, OUTSIDE the change lifecycle, to catch accumulated decay. This column is mostly empty today (gap G2).

statuses.partial: Exists but limited in scope, opt-in, or report-only rather than enforced.
statuses.planned: Identified in the gap analysis, not yet built. gap_ref points at the gap id.

sensor_tiers.minimal: Secrets + structural basics when configured; low-ceremony CLI/library work.
sensor_tiers.standard: Default product posture — historical pre-commit set (including sprout-diff).
sensor_tiers.strict: Standard plus architecture ratchets at commit (cycle-detection, coupling-ratchet).

steering_loop.name: program.md
steering_loop.description: Karpathy human<->agent bridge; the steering input that biases /auto.

steering_loop.name: learned-rules.md
steering_loop.description: Monotonically growing failure-derived rules, injected into future agent prompts. The harness improving itself.

steering_loop.name: CLAUDE.md update suggestions
steering_loop.description: Stop hook surfaces session learnings as suggested CLAUDE.md edits (applied between sessions, never mid-run).

guides.description: 10 quality principles injected into every generator teammate: TDD, static typing, 30-line function cap, deep modules, explicit errors, latency budgets.
Bounded clarification gate — resolve ambiguity before code, consuming local context first.
Architecture reference copied into target projects.
Default layer order + roots that the layer sensor enforces; overridden per target project via project-manifest.json#architecture (opt-out for non-layered shapes).
BRD -> spec -> design artifacts + acceptance criteria + sprint contracts: the functional specification the agent builds against.

SPDD REASONS Canvas (gap G4): /design emits specs/design/reasons-canvas.md (Requirements/Entities/Approach/Structure/Operations/Norms/Safeguards + a machine-read Governs list). Living artifact — 'fix the prompt first' when behaviour changes; brownfield grounds entities existing-vs-new in the code-graph.
Behaviour-preservation skills (checking-coverage, pinning-down-behavior, sprouting, keeping-refactors-pure) that steer brownfield edits.

AT-first discipline (gap G20, Matteo Vaccari's 'Acceptance Tests for AI-Assisted Development'): before implementation on a story, writes a business-readable Given/When/Then acceptance test against a Ports-and-Adapters seam — reusing /seam-finder's observable/funnel/asymmetry scoring rather than inventing a new seam concept — with a test-double adapter standing in for I/O, confirms it fails for the right reason, then proceeds to implementation. Wired into /test (Step 4.6, part of the plan-phase deliverable) and /change Step S4. Additive to, not a replacement for, the existing Playwright E2E layer (/test Step 5), which remains the final full-stack confirmation layer — this closes the harness's structural instance of Vaccari's E2E-as-AT-implementation antipattern (E2E was previously the ONLY acceptance-criteria verification layer). As of gap G23, this discipline has real commit-time mechanical proof (at-first-gate.js + record-at-red.js, mirroring legacy-discipline-gate.js's G17 pattern): a story's new production files are blocked without both an AT file on disk and a receipt proving it ran red. Readability review remains prompt-level only (surfaced to code-reviewer at Step S6, not mechanically checked) — see HARNESS.md G20/G23 for the current, narrower disclosed scope.

App-level observability baseline (gap G9, guide-half): the generator emits a RED-metrics + /metrics + trace-id-log-correlation baseline INTO generated server apps, steered by observability-conventions.md (stack-neutral) + observability-python-fastapi.md (concrete). Default-on for server shapes via project-manifest.json#observability (scaffold-render.js); deploy adds Prometheus scrape discovery; /metrics is a verifiable acceptance criterion. The runtime-SLO drift sensor that reads these signals is a separate follow-on.

Per-topology harness templates (gap G10): /scaffold resolves a detected topology (web-app / api-service / cli-or-library) and presets a coherent bundle of existing manifest knobs (architecture, observability, verification, ceremony, model_tier) via the TOPOLOGIES registry, instead of a uniform default. Ashby's-Law variety reduction; drop-in extensible; the manifest stays per-project overridable.

Default-on advisory token governor: /scaffold creates placeholder living navigation for empty greenfield repos or a lean initial DeepWiki/code-map for source-bearing repos; agents are steered to wiki/symbol-map/context queries before broad source reads. Configured by project-manifest.json#token_governor.

Cross-sprint architecture invariants (sprint-delta lane): a human-owned, PR-reviewed file scaffolded into every project at specs/design/constitution.md. /design --delta's design-delta rubric treats every '## Invariants' line as a hard constraint on every future sprint's design amendment.

Bounded source-context retrieval (schema v2) over the living DeepWiki/code-map: lexical+wiki BM25-ish ranking, optional TF-IDF semantic index (.claude/state/nav-index), depth-2 graph neighbors, co-change expansion, optional --diff boost, impact-scoped tests_to_run, task_map + confidence, read_next citations, session receipt, and nav-telemetry.jsonl. Facade: nav-query.js; MCP: nav-mcp-server.js; change-family Iron Law requires pack before broad source reads.
Single CLI facade for pack/symbol/callers/impact/cochange/semantic/refresh over code-graph + secondary nav artifacts. MCP twin: nav-mcp-server.js.

guides.signal: stale or missing TF-IDF nav index after graph refresh
guides.description: Offline TF-IDF semantic index over symbols+wiki; rebuilt by graph-refresh / nav-query refresh; fail-open when missing. Feeds context-pack scoring.
Git co-change edges (specs/brownfield/co-change.json); expands context-pack neighbors beyond import graph.

guides.signal: stale concept pages when member hashes change
guides.description: Hash-cached deterministic concept pages under specs/brownfield/wiki/concepts/, steered by .harness/wiki.json.
Deterministic lean brownfield essays (codebase/architecture/test/risk/change-strategy) from code-graph — default /brownfield path skips LLM essays; --full for narrative enrichment.
Pure-JSON inverted index for O(1) symbol/caller lookup without re-walking the full graph; rebuilt on graph-refresh.

guides.signal: golden-query recall_rate and pack vs naive token estimates
guides.description: Golden navigation benchmark (test/fixtures/nav-bench/golden-queries.json); measures pack recall and estimated token savings.
Standalone tool-output compactor: preserves raw logs on disk, extracts failures/paths/lines, and emits raw/packed/saved token estimates. Hook integration remains future work.

Local Compress-Cache-Retrieve layer: stores raw command/search context by hash, emits compact packs with retrieval commands, and supports query-filtered raw retrieval through context-retrieve.js, run-compact.js, and search-compact.js.

First-context-window vs continuation split (gap G13): on the first /auto window (Session 0 only, no completed groups) the loop verifies the initializer's artifacts are coherent (populated features.json, executable init.sh, specs prerequisites) before building, instead of running identical recovery logic on window 1 and window N. Implements Anthropic's multi-context-window 'a different prompt for the very first context window' principle.

Bounded post-PR response loop: /pr-respond polls a harness-opened PR's CI checks and review comments, classifies failures via the /auto self-healing table, fixes/pushes/replies with evidence, and stops on clean/max-cycles/watch-window/budget. Opt-in from /build and /feature via --respond (default off); never merges or force-pushes — merge stays human-owned. The deterministic half (poll/diff/handled-state) lives in .claude/scripts/pr-poll.js.
Guide for resolving conflicts between sensors and recording reviewed waivers with explicit expiry, so a larger harness stays coherent instead of accumulating contradictory gates.

Prompt-level guide (gap G32, prompt-only like acceptance-tests-first's discipline half before G23 added its gate): when a single mechanical fix — a /refactor principle applied across a --sweep --auto-fix batch, or a major dependency bump's call-site rewrite — spans more than ~10 files, apply it to a small trial batch (3 files, or the smallest representative sample) first, prove it with tests/lint/typecheck, and only then extend the same edit to the rest. Wired into refactor/SKILL.md Step 5 and upgrading-dependencies/SKILL.md Step 6 (locked by test/canary-rollout-wiring.test.js, same skill-text-assertion pattern test/gate-reverify-wiring.test.js uses for a prompt-only feature with no runtime script). No computational sensor: the file-count trigger is judgment the agent applies mid-task, not a mechanically checkable commit-time property — a canary failure is caught by the SAME test/lint/typecheck gates every other step already runs, not a new one.

Solo-vs-team policy for multi-story groups: solo | solo_sequential | team based on ownership span and cross-story deps. Reduces multi-agent boundary tax on tiny independent stories. Consumed by generator Rule 2.
Frontier mid-run advisor (Opus, read-only): structural re-rank/unblock at fixed checkpoints (post_fail_2, mid_epic) or /advise. Not self-serve escalation. Cap via execution.advisor_max_per_run.
Claude-only skill that loads docs/prompting-standards.md when creating or editing agents, skills, or commands (trigger conditions, model-agnostic bodies, no reasoning-as-text).

sensors.signal: lint violations (complexity, length, style)
sensors.description: ruff/eslint on the saved file (report-only on save, blocking at /auto Gate 2 and pre-commit). Block messages are enriched per-rule via lib/sensor-guidance (gap G5): each flagged rule gets an agent-facing fix line + the threshold-bump-with-justification valve ('positive prompt injection').

sensors.signal: type errors
sensors.description: mypy per-file on save (block messages enriched per-rule via lib/sensor-guidance, gap G5); tsc --noEmit project-wide at pre-commit.

sensors.signal: 30-line function / 300-line file violations
sensors.description: Pre-disk caps enforced by pre-write-gate.

sensors.signal: an Edit/MultiEdit's changed line range overlaps an UNCOVERED symbol per coverage_map.py
sensors.description: Real-time, PreToolUse half of checking-coverage-before-change's Iron Law (predates this session's G17 work; found unregistered during the G17 review and registered here rather than left orphaned). For a production file the brownfield code-graph maps, blocks an Edit/MultiEdit whose changed lines overlap an UNCOVERED symbol range, routing to pinning-down-behavior or sprouting-instead-of-editing; missing coverage data blocks with the scoped-regen command. Verdicts are cached per file against the graph/coverage file mtimes so coverage_map.py only re-runs when either changed. Tooling gaps (no python3, regex-fallback graph with no per-file symbol records) degrade to a non-blocking note, never a silent pass. Escape hatch: HARNESS_COVERAGE_PREFLIGHT=off. Composes with, does not duplicate, legacy-discipline-proof (G17): this sensor blocks the EDIT itself in real time inside one session; G17 separately proves at COMMIT time that the check actually happened and, for an UNCOVERED symbol, that pin-down/sprout evidence was staged — an agent could in principle disable this session-time gate (HARNESS_COVERAGE_PREFLIGHT=off) for one edit and still be caught by G17 at commit time, and vice versa a commit assembled outside a live session (no PreToolUse hook fired) is still caught by G17's receipt check.

sensors.signal: coverage below baseline or 80% floor
sensors.description: Repo-wide coverage ratchet (monotonic) at pre-commit and /auto Gate 3.

sensors.signal: per-diff coverage below floor
sensors.description: Coverage floor applied to only the files a group changed.

sensors.signal: SOLID breaks, god functions, duplication, swallowed errors
sensors.description: Inferential structure review on changed files (structure lens of the merged code-reviewer agent).

sensors.signal: surviving mutants (tests that pass but do not bite)
sensors.description: Diff-scoped mutation gate (gap G7): the pre-commit hook runs mutation-smoke over a group's changed production files during /auto builds; survivors below threshold BLOCK with the file:line + exact flip. Also runnable standalone (npm run mutation) and still used by pinning-down-behavior / the brownfield CR lane.

sensors.signal: hubs, cycles, instability, dead-code candidates
sensors.description: AST-derived coupling/dead-code report. Its unstable-hub signal (fan_in/instability) is now ALSO enforced at commit time via the coupling-ratchet gate (gap G18, coupling-gate.js) — see that sensor entry. The report's own `hubs` table stays capped to the top 25 by fan-in, a deliberate display choice for a human-readable markdown table; gap G26 fixed the bug where enforcement consumers (coupling-ratchet among them) were reusing that same capped list for a threshold check, so a real unstable hub ranked 26th+ by fan-in was structurally invisible to the ratchet even though it never belonged in the display table's cap in the first place — those consumers now read the separate, uncapped `metrics.unstable_hubs` field instead. The report itself remains discovery-only — informational, not enforced — for everything else it carries: the full fan-in/fan-out tables and dead-code candidates.

sensors.signal: root nouns recurring across 2+ symbols in code-graph.json
sensors.description: Deterministic evidence extraction (mirrors modularity-pack.js's split) for brownfield Step 6: surfaces candidate domain terms with file evidence for the LLM to confirm into CONTEXT.md, instead of open-ended judgment on a full source read.

sensors.signal: stale or missing DeepWiki/code-map navigation after source edits
sensors.description: Stop/SubagentStop refresh patches code-graph.json, re-renders symbol-map.md, and re-renders the deterministic DeepWiki. Placeholder greenfield graphs are upgraded to real graphs on the first source write; /status surfaces freshness and estimated token savings.

sensors.signal: hubs (pre-classified legit vs suspicious), cycles, duplication candidates
sensors.description: Deterministic grounding for the modularity review (gap G6): extracts evidence from code-graph.json and pre-tags likely-legitimate hubs (factories/schemas/utils) so the inferential pass doesn't flag intentional patterns as god modules.

sensors.signal: semantic duplication, misplaced responsibility, argument clumps, coupling imbalance
sensors.description: Inferential modularity review (gap G6) grounded in the modularity-pack. Manually/human-triggered only, via /brownfield --full Step 3.6 or /design --delta Step D3.5 (scoped to the amendment) — it has no periodic component of its own; the drift-cadence proxy for staleness between real runs is the separate modularity-review-staleness sensor (G19). Judges duplication/responsibility/clumps/cycles against source, confirms legit hubs, and (gap G27) applies Vlad Khononov's published Balanced Coupling rubric — classifies each hub/duplication relationship's Integration Strength, Distance, and Volatility and flags coupling-imbalance only for the high-strength + far-distance + high-volatility combination via BALANCE = (STRENGTH XOR DISTANCE) OR NOT VOLATILITY. A maintainability sensor, not a gate.

sensors.signal: a currently-unstable hub absent from the last real modularity review's marker snapshot
sensors.description: Gap G19: modularity-review was previously mislabeled cadence:drift even though it has no periodic trigger of its own (fixed above). This sensor is the genuinely periodic proxy that was missing: record-modularity-review.js writes .claude/state/modularity-review-marker.json ({timestamp, unstableHubIds}) after a real review completes (/brownfield --full Step 3.6 or /design --delta Step D3.5); drift-report.js's withModularityStaleness (hooks/lib/drift.js) diffs the live code-graph's current unstable hubs against that marker and flags any that are new since the last real review. No marker at all (never run) means every currently unstable hub counts as stale. A cheap, deterministic nudge that the expensive inferential review is overdue — not a substitute for it.

sensors.signal: upward layer-import violations
sensors.description: One-way HORIZONTAL layered-import enforcement on every write and at pre-commit. The vertical complement is bounded-context-rules (gap G8).

sensors.signal: a change added an import cycle
sensors.description: Import-cycle ratchet (gap G8): cycle-gate.js compares the code-graph cycle count to a monotonic baseline (.claude/state/cycle-baseline.txt). A new cycle BLOCKS (/auto Gate 4, /gate, npm run cycles); removing cycles ratchets the baseline down. Detection still also feeds coupling-report.md + the drift monitor.

sensors.signal: a change added an unstable hub (fan_in >= 5, instability >= 0.8)
sensors.description: Unstable-hub ratchet (gap G18): coupling-gate.js reads via drift.js's hubsForStabilityCheck (gap G26: prefers the uncapped code-graph.json metrics.unstable_hubs field when present, so a hub ranked 26th+ by fan-in in the top-25-truncated metrics.hubs display list is still caught — falls back to the prior unstableHubIds(metrics.hubs) behavior on an older graph) — the same fan_in >= 5 / instability >= 0.8 thresholds coupling-report.md and the drift-architecture sensor already use — and cycle-gate.js's gateDecision ratchet math to compare the unstable-hub set to a monotonic baseline (.claude/state/coupling-baseline.txt, which stores the hub-id set so a BLOCK can name the specific new hub(s), not just a count). A new unstable hub BLOCKS (/auto Gate 4, /gate, npm run coupling-gate) with fan-in/instability numbers and concrete extract-a-narrower-interface/split-responsibilities remediation — an agent-actionable message, not just a fact statement, per the Fowler 'Maintainability Sensors for Coding Agents' good-prompt-injection principle. Removing unstable hubs ratchets the baseline down. Closes the gap where this signal previously only surfaced on the drift cadence (npm run drift) or in the on-demand coupling-report.md, never fed back to the agent at commit time. No code-graph yet → skipped loudly, same degrade pattern as cycle-detection (G8).

sensors.signal: cross-context internal-import violations
sensors.description: Vertical bounded-context rules (gap G8): a context may not reach into another's internals except via its public surface or a declared allow-edge. Opt-in via project-manifest.json#architecture.contexts; enforced on every write (verify-on-save) and at pre-commit. The ArchUnit/dependency-cruiser rule the horizontal layer check can't express.

sensors.signal: API response/shape violations
sensors.description: Sprint-contract + api-contracts.schema.json validation; evaluator Layer 1 checks live responses.

sensors.signal: breaking changes to an existing API's OpenAPI contract
sensors.description: Contract-drift gate (gap G12): contract-drift-gate.js runs `oasdiff breaking` between the OpenAPI spec at the git base and the working tree, BLOCKing on breaking changes. Boundary-gated in /gate (fires when the OpenAPI spec changes) + `npm run contract-drift`. Conditional on an OpenAPI spec existing; degrades loudly (exit 0) when oasdiff is unprovisioned. Exit-code gating only (version-robust).

sensors.signal: p95 latency regression vs baseline
sensors.description: Read-endpoint p95 ratchet; regression FAILs, absolute-budget overrun WARNs.

sensors.signal: failing tests
sensors.description: pytest/vitest/jest at /auto Gate 1 and pre-commit.

sensors.signal: API contract failures against running app
sensors.description: Evaluator Layer 1: curl + jsonschema against the live application.

sensors.signal: broken user flows
sensors.description: Evaluator Layer 2: Playwright browser interaction with semantic selectors.

sensors.signal: weak frontend quality (design/originality/craft/functionality)
sensors.description: Evaluator Layer 3 vision scoring (Full mode); secondary GAN loop with plateau detection.

sensors.signal: logic errors, missing edge cases, contract breaks
sensors.description: Fresh-context correctness review (reads only the diff, never the builder's conversation; correctness lens of the merged code-reviewer agent).

sensors.signal: OWASP Top 10 findings + dependency CVEs
sensors.description: Adversarial find-then-refute review + npm audit. Boundary-gated in /gate. Inferential only — no computational SAST (gap G3).

sensors.signal: credential-shaped strings
sensors.description: Always-available baseline regex secrets scan: pre-write (lib/secrets.js) on every edit AND pre-commit (checkSecrets) over all staged files. gitleaks is the enhanced tier, run at /gate via security-scan.js when provisioned.

sensors.signal: known vulnerable code patterns
sensors.description: Semgrep SAST via security-scan.js --sast, boundary-gated in /gate as the computational complement to the inferential security-reviewer. Degrades loudly when semgrep is unprovisioned (like eslint/tsc).

sensors.signal: known-vulnerable dependencies (CVEs)
sensors.description: npm audit + pip-audit via security-scan.js --deps, run at /gate. Completes the computational security set; npm audit was previously only inside the inferential security-reviewer.

sensors.signal: axe/WCAG violations
sensors.description: axe-core gate (gap G12): default-on for UI stories. contract-accessibility-default.js injects a default accessibility_checks block into any contract with playwright_checks (opt out via project-manifest accessibility.enabled:false; explicit blocks respected); the evaluator then runs axe and FAILs serious/critical impacts in Full mode (failure_layer:accessibility), WARN in Lean. Gate logic lives in evaluate/SKILL.md.

sensors.signal: net-new or dropped requirements vs FRD or confirmed interview spine
sensors.description: Deterministic hard block: the BRD may only contain content traced to the FRD or interview requirements spine, or the clarification log.

sensors.signal: untraced stories, dropped BRs, orphan/uncovered tests
sensors.description: Deterministic hard block at spec (story vs BRD) and test (case vs AC + obligation).

sensors.signal: entity/model definition names in domain_concepts, data-models.schema.json, or api-contracts.schema.json that do not resolve to a CONTEXT.md term
sensors.description: Deterministic vocabulary-consistency sensor: extends the traceability axis from ID-linkage (trace-check) to term-linkage. Catches 'Account in the BRD, User in the API contract' before code is written. CONTEXT.md terms may originate from domain_concepts (Step 2.8) or, for any enabled vertical plugin registered in vertical-glossary-packs.json, from vertical-glossary-pack.js (Step 2.7) — vocabulary-check.js validates either origin identically.

sensors.signal: BRD/story acceptance criteria without required unit/API/E2E evidence
sensors.description: Verification matrix gate: a deterministic BRD-to-test-to-runtime conformance control. Runs in planning, sprint-contract, implementation, and pre-PR executed phases, requiring every implementation-ready acceptance criterion to have the required verification layers and evidence. The executed phase is additionally re-run by the pre-commit hook (.claude/git-hooks/pre-commit, checkVerificationMatrix) as a deterministic commit-time backstop, so, during an active sprint group with source files staged, skipping the in-session invocation cannot bypass it.

sensors.signal: schema obligations without a negative test
sensors.description: Mines minLength/required/enum from design schemas into test obligations.

sensors.signal: low-confidence plan band
sensors.description: Confidence-gated planning before execution. After /design+/test plan-only, plan-confidence.js writes specs/plan-confidence.json; --gate exits 2 when band is low so /build --auto must /clarify once or stop (never builds blind). Lite --auto escalates to full --auto on exit 2.

sensors.signal: no clean cut-point for a brownfield change
sensors.description: Stops or surfaces at a gate when no safe seam exists for the goal.

sensors.signal: new import cycles or newly-unstable hubs vs last snapshot
sensors.description: Continuous monitor (gap G2): diffs code-graph cycle/hub metrics against specs/drift/drift-snapshot.json and flags regressions. Run by any cadence (npm run drift, /schedule, /loop, CI); exit 1 on new drift.

sensors.signal: newly-orphaned (dead-code) files vs last snapshot
sensors.description: Continuous monitor (gap G2): diffs the code-map orphan set so dead code that accumulates outside the change lifecycle is surfaced, not silently grown.

sensors.signal: new dependency CVEs in unchanged code
sensors.description: Continuous monitor (gap G2): re-runs npm/pip audit (reusing security-scan runDeps) and flags CVEs newly disclosed against dependencies the change lifecycle never touched.

sensors.signal: REASONS Canvas missing a section or an empty Governs list
sensors.description: Deterministic structure gate (gap G4) run in /design Step 1.9: the Canvas must carry all seven REASONS sections and a non-empty Governs list. Hard block.

sensors.signal: Canvas governs a source path that no longer exists
sensors.description: Design-vs-code drift (gap G4): the drift monitor reads reasons-canvas.md's Governs list and flags governed paths that vanished — the living design referencing deleted/moved code. Pairs with the 'fix the prompt first' discipline.

sensors.signal: app fails to boot / health-check on a fresh-process resume
sensors.description: Startup smoke check (gap G14): the first time /auto enters SECTION 2 in a fresh process with prior committed work, it boots the app (project-manifest.json#verification.mode) and runs the evaluator Health-Check Retry loop BEFORE selecting work, catching a tree a killed prior window left broken that the append-only progress log never recorded. Failure routes to SECTION 6 self-healing as failure_layer:infrastructure. Implements Anthropic's 'run a basic test on the dev server at session start' principle.

sensors.signal: 5xx error-rate over SLO; p95 over absolute budget
sensors.description: Runtime-SLO sensor (gap G9 sensor-half): scrapes the generated app's RED /metrics (the guide-half's http_requests_total + http_request_duration_seconds) and compares to project-manifest.json#observability.slo. 5xx error-rate over error_rate_pct FAILs /evaluate in Full mode (failure_layer:slo); p95 over p95_ms is a WARN (regression stays with the perf ratchet). Wired into /evaluate Step P4 (app already booted) and runnable standalone (npm run slo -- --url <live>) for the drift cadence.

sensors.signal: snapshot oracle modified or added without re-approval
sensors.description: Approved-fixtures gate (gap G12): tracks a baseline of approved snapshot files (path+sha256 in specs/test_artefacts/approved-snapshots.json) and BLOCKs in /gate when an approved snapshot's checksum changed or a new unapproved snapshot appears; approve-fixtures.js (npm run approve-fixtures) re-blesses. Dormant (exit 0) when a project has no snapshot files. Stops agents silently regenerating oracles to pass tests.

sensors.signal: tests that pass and fail across repeated runs
sensors.description: Flake detection (gap G12; e2e mode added gap G28): flake-detector.js runs a test command N times (npm run flakes), parses node:test TAP per run, and reports tests that both passed and failed across runs. As of gap G28, a new --e2e flag switches it to run a Playwright command per run and parse its --reporter=json output via hooks/lib/regression-gate.js's extractPlaywrightResults (reused, not reimplemented), so it can also populate specs/drift/flake-history.jsonl with Playwright-shaped 'describe > test' titles — closing regression-suite-full (G15)'s disclosed limitation that e2e-spec auto-quarantine never actually matched anything. Drift cadence — opt-in / /schedule, non-blocking (exit 1 on flakes for cron/CI signal); deliberately NOT a /gate or /auto gate, since a genuine flake should not block the change lifecycle. Errored/timed-out/unparseable runs are excluded from aggregation in both modes.

sensors.signal: changed files are missing from the REASONS Canvas Governs or Operations sections
sensors.description: Living-design sync sensor: compares changed files with specs/design/reasons-canvas.md so design-governed changes update the Canvas first instead of letting SPDD artifacts silently rot. Runnable as npm run canvas-sync.

sensors.signal: changed source files owned by no story in component-map.md
sensors.description: Deterministic file-ownership sensor: staged/changed source files must be owned by a story in specs/design/component-map.md ('no story, no code'). Enforced at pre-commit (checkOwnership, HARNESS_OWNERSHIP_GATE=off escape) and at /gate (sensor-waiver escape); a map that parses to zero entries fails loudly (empty_map) instead of passing vacuously.

sensors.signal: a commit changes specs/design/ with no matching record under specs/design/amendments/
sensors.description: Sprint-delta lane sensor: once a design baseline exists (specs/design/architecture.md tracked at HEAD), any commit touching specs/design/ (excluding the amendments/ dir itself) must carry a new file under specs/design/amendments/ in the same commit — otherwise a sprint's design evolution is invisible to the next sprint's human gate. Enforced at pre-commit (HARNESS_AMENDMENT_GATE=off escape); the very first design-authoring commit is exempt (verdict initial-design).

sensors.signal: a /feature story touches >3 files, a risk category (auth/payments/persistence/public-API), or introduces a new module
sensors.description: Sprint-delta lane routing sensor: classifies a single /feature story as architecturally invisible (-> /change, no design amendment) or design-touching (-> /design --delta, amendment + GATE 2), so small stories don't pay full design-gate overhead and design-relevant ones don't skip it. Advisory — always exits 0, it routes work rather than blocking it.

sensors.signal: invalid or expired sensor waiver
sensors.description: Validates specs/reviews/sensor-waivers.json so threshold bumps and suppressions are reviewed, complete, and expire.

sensors.signal: tests that repeatedly flake across drift runs
sensors.description: Aggregates flake-detector reports into specs/drift/flake-history.jsonl and renders recurring flaky tests by frequency.

sensors.signal: Stryker/mutmut survivors in critical modules
sensors.description: Optional release/critical-module mutation tier. Detects Stryker or mutmut when configured, exits unprovisioned when absent, and never replaces the fast mutation-smoke gate. As of gap G25, /scaffold gives JS/TS and Python stacks a real onboarding path: it writes a project-manifest.json#quality.mutation block and a starter stryker.conf.json or pyproject.toml [tool.mutmut] section (root-level, matching detectTool()'s root-only lookup), so a fresh project is provisioned by default instead of permanently unprovisioned. deep-mutation.js itself is unchanged; older pre-G25 projects and non-JS/Python stacks still exit unprovisioned until configured manually.

sensors.signal: context_search_skipped or broad_source_read or verbose_command token warning or block
sensors.description: Token optimizer hook for Read|Bash. Advisory (default): warns when context_search_required and no fresh context-pack receipt (context_search_skipped), on avoidable broad source reads when symbol ranges exist, and on likely verbose commands that should use run-compact.js. Enforced mode (token_governor.mode=enforced): same predicates block with exit 2; fails open without graph ranges/placeholder. Escape: HARNESS_TOKEN_GOVERNOR=off.

sensors.signal: est $ / model mix / token totals from run receipts
sensors.description: Operator cost report over .claude/runs receipts: est/measured $, agents by role, model mix, optional cache-read share. Writes .claude/state/cost-report.json; /status Cost line via pipeline-state-readers#readCostSummary.

sensors.signal: a previously-passing accumulated e2e/ Playwright spec or a prior story-group's sprint-contract API check now fails against the running app
sensors.description: Cross-feature regression gate (gap G15): /evaluate and /gate only verify the CURRENT story-group's sprint contract, and /change and /vibe only re-run the unit suite — neither re-ran the accumulated e2e/ Playwright suite or prior groups' sprint contracts, so a change could pass its own tests while silently breaking an earlier feature. regression-gate.js re-runs every *.spec.(ts|js) under e2e/ via the project's e2e command and re-executes every prior group's sprint-contract contract.api_checks as live HTTP requests, first re-validating each contract's schema via the same hooks/lib/contract-schema machinery validate-contract.js uses. Tests already quarantined in specs/drift/flake-history.jsonl are excluded by matching the recorded `name` field against the Playwright spec's joined `describe > test` title or the api_check's id/description. Wired as a hard block into /gate and /auto's pre-merge-to-WAVE_BASE step (the full, unabridged sweep, reserved for merge time since it's too expensive to run on every local iteration); degrades loudly (never silently) when there is no e2e/ or sprint-contracts/ baseline yet. As of gap G16, /change Step S5 and /vibe Step 6 run the faster impact-scoped-regression sensor instead on every iteration, with this full sweep remaining the mandatory merge-time backstop. As of gap G28, flake-history.jsonl's `name` field can be populated with Playwright-shaped `describe > test` titles via flake-detector.js's new --e2e mode (previously it only ever recorded node:test TAP output, so this quarantine match path never actually fired) — see the flake-detection sensor entry. api_check quarantine (matched on id/description) was unaffected either way.

sensors.signal: an impact-scoped e2e spec or prior story-group's sprint-contract API check now fails against the running app
sensors.description: Fast local complement to gap G15 (regression-suite-full): running the WHOLE accumulated e2e/ suite and every prior contract on every single /change or /vibe iteration is too expensive, and a single non-peer-reviewed preprint (TDAD, arXiv:2603.17973 — one finding, not its own proposed fix, survived adversarial verification) found merely instructing an agent to 'run more tests' without targeted context made regressions worse, not better — directional evidence for impact-aware scoping, not a settled result. local-regression-gate.js instead computes a deterministic Test Impact Analysis via impact-scope.js: changed files -> reverse-dependency blast radius over code-graph.json's imports/calls edges -> owning story-group(s) (specs/test_artefacts/verification-matrix.json primary, specs/design/component-map.md + features.json fallback) -> that group's e2e spec(s) + sprint-contract, unioned with any human-curated project-manifest.json#verification.golden_paths (an always-on safety net under a possibly-stale graph). Reuses hooks/lib/regression-gate.js's primitives (runE2eSuite now accepts an optional specFiles filter, extractPlaywrightFailures, isQuarantined, regressPriorContract) rather than duplicating them. Tests already quarantined in specs/drift/flake-history.jsonl are excluded; an unreadable contract-schema BLOCKs (never silently validates against an empty schema). Wired as a hard block into /change Step S5 and /vibe Step 6 (every iteration); /gate and /auto's pre-merge step keep running G15's full unabridged sweep unchanged as the merge-time backstop, since impact analysis over a possibly-stale graph is deliberately treated as weaker coverage than the full suite, not a replacement for it.

sensors.signal: a staged MODIFIED production source file has no recorded checking-coverage-before-change verdict covering the actually-changed line range, or a recorded UNCOVERED verdict for that range with no RELATED pin-down/sprout test staged in the same commit
sensors.description: Mechanical proof for the legacy-preservation discipline (gap G17; range/relatedness-tightened by gap G29): checking-coverage-before-change, pinning-down-behavior, and sprouting-instead-of-editing were prompt-level instructions only, with no sensor confirming they ran — an agent could skip straight to editing an uncovered legacy symbol and nothing would block it. Directional evidence this is the exact failure mode procedural-only discipline invites under pressure: a single non-peer-reviewed preprint (TDAD, arXiv:2603.17973) found instructing an agent without a mechanical, presence-checkable gate can make outcomes WORSE, not better. record-coverage-verdict.js wraps checking-coverage-before-change Step 2's (unmodified) coverage_map.py call, appending every symbol verdict to specs/reviews/coverage-verdicts.jsonl as a receipt (path/symbol/start/end/verdict/tests/recordedAt) while passing the JSON through unchanged so the skill's existing read-and-route flow is undisturbed. legacy-discipline-gate.js then re-checks every staged MODIFIED (not newly-added — a brand-new file is greenfield) production source file at commit time: no receipt covering the ACTUALLY-CHANGED lines (gap G29 Gap A — parsed from a real `git diff --cached -U0` via hooks/lib/diff-hunks.js; a receipt for a different symbol/range in the same file no longer counts) BLOCKs (the Iron Law was never run for this edit), and a latest-recorded UNCOVERED verdict for that range BLOCKs unless a RELATED new/modified test-shaped file is also staged (gap G29 Gap B — relatedness via hooks/lib/legacy-discipline-relatedness.js: component-map.md story ownership, then a naming heuristic, then a disclosed commit-wide fallback, not 'any test file anywhere in the commit') — composing with, not duplicating, mutation-gate.js (G7)'s independent proof that new test code actually bites. As of gap G29, a manual (non-/auto) commit that passes the UNCOVERED-evidence path also gets a narrow bite-check backstop (hooks/lib/legacy-bite-check.js, reusing mutation-gate.js's runMutationOnFiles with a much smaller budget than G7's own — 3 mutants/10s vs 12/30s — scoped only to the file(s) that just passed via evidence); it skips when inAutoBuild (G7 already re-verifies there) or HARNESS_LEGACY_BITE_CHECK=off. Reuses ownership-check.js's SOURCE_EXTS/isSource classification and hooks/lib/tdd.js's isTestFile rather than reinventing either. Degrades loudly (never silently blocks forever) when no specs/brownfield/code-graph.json exists yet, or when the graph has no per-file symbol records (regex-fallback producer — the same condition coverage_map.py itself exits 3 on); --files CLI mode (no git diff plumbing) falls back to the pre-G29 whole-file assumption, disclosed not silent. HARNESS_LEGACY_DISCIPLINE_GATE=off acknowledges a local skip (default-on, unlike keeping-refactors-pure's opt-in HARNESS_COMMIT_KIND arming); a real exception belongs in specs/reviews/sensor-waivers.json per docs/sensor-arbitration.md. KNOWN LIMITATIONS (disclosed, see HARNESS.md G29): a pure-deletion diff hunk is approximated as a single-point changed range, not an exact line-level proof; the naming-heuristic/commit-wide-fallback relatedness tiers are real but weaker signals than component-map.md story ownership; the bite-check backstop's budget is intentionally narrower than G7's own and only runs for a manual commit.

sensors.signal: a story with a staged NEW (added) production source file has no specs/test_artefacts/acceptance/{story-id}.<ext> on disk, or no matching record-at-red.js receipt naming that {storyId, atPath} pair
sensors.description: Mechanical proof for the AT-first discipline (gap G20's disclosed forward item, closed by gap G23): writing-acceptance-tests-first's Process step 5 ('confirm it fails for the right reason') was a prompt-level instruction only, with no sensor confirming an AT was actually written and observed red before a story's implementation shipped. record-at-red.js runs a story's AT test command and, only when it FAILS (the AT is red), appends a receipt {storyId, atPath, observedRedAt, testCmd} to specs/reviews/at-red-receipts.jsonl (append-only, gitignored, same convention as coverage-verdicts.jsonl); a PASSING run records nothing and exits non-zero instead, quoting the skill's own Red Flag so the non-receipt is visible in the calling step's output. at-first-gate.js then re-checks at commit time: for every story with a NEW (added, not modified) staged production source file, it requires BOTH the acceptance-test file and a matching receipt, or BLOCKs, naming the story and the exact remediation. Determines 'which story owns this new file' by reusing parseComponentMapStoryFiles from hooks/lib/impact-scope.js (gap G16) — the codebase's existing story->files component-map.md parser — rather than ownership-check.js's own parseComponentMap, which flattens every row into one owned-path Set with no story association; a brand-new file with no story owner at all remains ownership-check.js's own, already-covered problem, not this gate's concern. Wired as a hard block into .claude/git-hooks/pre-commit, default-on (HARNESS_AT_FIRST_GATE=off acknowledges a local skip, mirroring HARNESS_LEGACY_DISCIPLINE_GATE=off's shape); a real exception belongs in specs/reviews/sensor-waivers.json per docs/sensor-arbitration.md. Degrades loudly, never silently, when no specs/design/component-map.md exists yet or no staged NEW production file resolves to a story owner. KNOWN LIMITATION (disclosed, see HARNESS.md G23): a receipt's timestamp mechanically proves the AT was confirmed red by commit time, not that it strictly preceded every line of the later implementation diff — proving strict chronological ordering would need fragile git-history archaeology this gate does not attempt.

sensors.signal: a legacy file legacy-discipline-gate.js already classified as UNCOVERED-with-evidence, where that evidence is a genuinely NEW production file (a sprout, not a pin-down), has a staged diff intersecting more than 2 distinct code-graph.json symbol ranges
sensors.description: Mechanical proof for sprouting-instead-of-editing's Iron Law (gap G17's disclosed forward item, closed by gap G30): 'touch the legacy file at exactly one call line (or the rename pair for wrap)' was a prose-only instruction, with nothing checking a legacy diff's actual symbol span. Fires only when legacy-discipline-gate.js (G17/G29) already sees a legacy file as UNCOVERED-with-evidence AND hooks/lib/sprout-classify.js's classifySprout (reusing legacy-discipline-relatedness.js's storyOwnersFor, G29) determines that evidence is a SPROUT — a genuinely new production file staged (diff-filter=A, source, non-test) alongside the legacy touch — rather than a PIN-DOWN (test files only, no new production file), which has no one-symbol constraint of its own. For each sprout-classified file, hooks/lib/sprout-symbol-check.js's symbolsTouchedByRanges checks the file's actually-changed line ranges (legacy-discipline-gate.js's gitDiffRanges/hooks/lib/diff-hunks.js, G29, reused) against code-graph.json's per-file symbols, flattening a class's methods to leaf units the same way coverage_map.py's symbol_rows already does. More than 2 distinct symbols touched BLOCKs, naming the extras; the cap is 2, not 1, because sprouting-instead-of-editing's own Process step 2 names a legitimate 2-symbol wrap-rename-pair shape — this gate does not attempt to mechanically distinguish a 1-symbol sprout from a 2-symbol wrap (judged too heuristic to build with confidence), so a 2-symbol touch passes with an 'assumed wrap-rename pair, not independently verified' note rather than a false-confident PASS. Wired as a hard block into .claude/git-hooks/pre-commit (checkSproutDiffGate, right after checkLegacyDisciplineGate), default-on (HARNESS_SPROUT_DIFF_GATE=off acknowledges a local skip, mirroring HARNESS_LEGACY_DISCIPLINE_GATE=off's shape); a real exception belongs in specs/reviews/sensor-waivers.json per docs/sensor-arbitration.md. Degrades loudly, never silently, mirroring legacy-discipline-gate.js's own noGraphVerdict/hasSymbolRecords conventions: no code-graph.json, a regex-fallback graph, no per-file symbol record for a candidate, or unavailable changed-range data (--files CLI mode) all SKIP with a note; no UNCOVERED-with-evidence candidate, or one present but pin-down-shaped, also SKIPs with a distinguishing note. KNOWN LIMITATIONS (disclosed, see HARNESS.md G30): reads code-graph.json as-is (the same staleness assumption G17/G29's receipt matching already accepts); the sprout-vs-pin-down classification's weakest tier (commit-wide-fallback, no component-map.md story link) is a commit-wide, not per-line-attributed, signal; the 2-symbol wrap-pair allowance is a cap, not a verified rename-pattern match.

sensors.signal: swallowed exceptions, empty catches, unstructured logs, boundary without logger, middleware without request_id
sensors.description: Static observability + exception-handling ratchet on changed production source (P1 human-trust program). Complements runtime-slo (G9) and code-gen observability conventions. Wired into /gate Step 2.5; BLOCK on bare except/empty catch; WARN on f-string logs and boundary-without-logger. Writes specs/reviews/observability-verdict.json.

sensors.signal: N+1 query-in-loop, unbounded loads, sequential independent awaits, sync-in-async
sensors.description: Diff-scoped static performance smell gate (P3). Complements hybrid perf-ratchet (runtime p95). BLOCK on N+1-in-loop and sync-in-async; WARN on unbounded loads and sequential awaits. Wired into /gate Step 2.5; writes specs/reviews/perf-smell-verdict.json.

sensors.signal: missing or failing aggregated gate receipt before PR
sensors.description: Human trust receipt (P0): aggregates evaluator, code-review, security, observability, perf-smell, regression, ownership into specs/reviews/quality-card.{md,json} and stamps .claude/state/gate-receipt.json. Always written at /gate Step 4; pr-body.js --require-gate refuses PR open when pass=false.

sensors.signal: unordered PR diffs without human logical tour
sensors.description: Devin Review–class logical walkthrough (P0/P2): groups changed files entry→domain→service→data→adapter→test, attaches code-review severity, graph blast radius, 5-minute human review script. Writes specs/reviews/walkthrough.{md,json}; embedded in PR body via pr-body.js.

sensors.signal: missing or stale docs/CODEBASE.md human homepage
sensors.description: Human-facing DeepWiki homepage (P0/P1): regenerates docs/CODEBASE.md from code-graph + CONTEXT + concept/wiki pages. Fail-open from graph-refresh secondary scripts; also /gate Step 4.

sensors.signal: human orientation without opening the source tree
sensors.description: Human Ask CLI (P2, Devin Ask analogue): wraps context-pack into markdown answer with citations and read_next. npm run ask -- "question"; writes specs/reviews/ask-last.md.

sensors.signal: stale quality-card, low active readiness pillars, missing human homepage
sensors.description: Weekly/ops digest (P3) over agent-readiness pillars + quality-card age + human-codebase presence. Report-only; npm run readiness-digest; writes specs/reviews/readiness-digest.{md,json}.

sensors.signal: a staged commit deletes a test-shaped file that had live test cases, decreases a test-shaped file's live test-case count, or adds a new skip/todo marker without decreasing the count
sensors.description: G7 (mutation-gate), G17 (legacy-discipline), and G30 (sprout-diff) all prove a change didn't silently regress PRODUCTION code; none of them catch the adjacent failure mode a refactor or dependency bump under pressure can reach for instead: making the suite pass by deleting, or newly skipping, the test that would have caught the regression, rather than fixing the code. hooks/lib/test-deletion-gate.js's classifyTestFileChange compares each staged test-shaped file's (hooks/lib/tdd.js isTestFile) old (git show HEAD:<file>) and new (git show :<file>, the staged index content) bodies with heuristic regex counts (same tier as secrets.js's baseline scan and tdd.js's own isTestFile — not an AST parse): a live-test-marker count (it(/test(/it.each(/test.each(/def test_...) that drops, or a deletion of a file that had live markers, is a hard finding; a same-or-higher test count but a newly added skip/todo marker (.skip(/.todo(/xit(/xdescribe(/xtest(/@pytest.mark.skip/@unittest.skip) is a second, independent finding kind. scripts/test-deletion-gate.js does the git plumbing (collectStagedChanges, same --diff-filter=M/D DI-exec pattern legacy-discipline-gate.js and sprout-diff-gate.js use) and exposes both a --staged CLI (npm run test-deletion-gate) and a checkStaged(exec) entry point the pre-commit hook calls directly. Wired into .claude/git-hooks/pre-commit as checkTestDeletionGate, deliberately called BEFORE the docs-only early exit (stagedFiles() filters --diff-filter=ACMR, which excludes pure deletions, so a commit that ONLY deletes a test file would otherwise never reach a check gated behind that filter) — the same reason checkSecrets and checkAmendmentProvenance already run there. Default-on hard block (HARNESS_TEST_DELETION_GATE=off acknowledges a local skip, mirroring HARNESS_LEGACY_DISCIPLINE_GATE=off's shape); a real exception (a test for removed functionality, a flaky test being quarantined) belongs in specs/reviews/sensor-waivers.json (sensor_id: test-deletion-guard) per docs/sensor-arbitration.md. Degrades loudly, never silently, when the sensor script is missing/unloadable (noteSkip, same convention as every other lazy-required pre-commit gate). KNOWN LIMITATIONS (disclosed, not hidden): heuristic regex counting, not an AST parse, so unusual formatting or dynamically generated test cases (a loop emitting it(...) calls) can mis-count; a test rewritten to cover different/broader behavior at the same or higher count is invisible to this gate (it proves the COUNT and SKIP STATE didn't regress, not that behavioral coverage is equivalent — that judgment stays with code-reviewer / the human); composes with, does not duplicate, regression-suite-full (G15) and impact-scoped-regression (G16), which prove a still-PRESENT test's outcome didn't regress, not that the test itself wasn't quietly removed or skipped.

sensors.signal: mid-session edit of CLAUDE.md, .mcp.json, or .claude/settings*.json
sensors.description: Blocks Write/Edit and Bash writes to prompt-cache prefix files so long sessions keep a stable cached prefix. Applies even in the harness monorepo (unlike machinery trust-boundary). Escape: HARNESS_PREFIX_EDIT=1 between sessions only.

drift_sensors_planned.note: The 'drift' cadence is the article's third column (repeatedly, slower, outside the change lifecycle). The architecture / dead-code / dependency-CVE signals are now SHIPPED via drift-report.js (see the drift-* sensors above). The runtime-SLO signal is now covered by the 'runtime-slo' integration sensor (slo-check.js): it ships as an integration-cadence sensor wired into /evaluate Step P4, and is also runnable standalone on a drift cadence (npm run slo -- --url <live>). No drift signals remain planned.

existing_drift_like.note: Watches the harness's OWN upstream (anthropics/claude-code), not the product codebase.
